title: Detect Suspicious Transaction Patterns
description: The system shall detect suspicious transaction patterns such as multiple rapid withdrawals.
rationale: To identify potential fraudulent activities and prevent financial loss by flagging transactions for manual review.
business_value: Improves fraud detection, reduces the risk of unauthorized transactions, and protects customers' financial security.

source.description: As a fraud analyst I need the system to detect suspicious transaction patterns such as multiple rapid withdrawals So that I can flag them for review and prevent potential fraud

acceptance_criteria: Given a user initiates multiple rapid withdrawals
When the system detects this pattern
Then the transactions are flagged for review
postconditions: Transactions that meet the suspicious pattern criteria are queued for manual review by fraud analysts.
functional_constraints: The system must monitor transaction frequency and speed in real-time.
Multiple rapid withdrawals within a predefined time frame should trigger a detection mechanism.
security_requirements: All transactions, including flagged ones, must be securely stored for audit purposes.
Fraud analysts must have appropriate access controls to review flagged transactions.

non_functional_requirements.performance: The system must respond within 5 seconds or less to detect suspicious patterns.
non_functional_requirements.availability: The system should be available 99.9% of the time.
non_functional_requirements.scalability: The system must be able to handle up to 1,000 transactions per minute during peak times.
non_functional_requirements.usability: The user interface for reviewing flagged transactions must be intuitive and error-free.

edge_cases: What happens if the transaction volume exceeds the system's capacity?
How does the system handle transactions that occur simultaneously from multiple locations?
What are the implications of a network outage on detecting and flagging rapid withdrawals?

interfaces.name: Transaction Monitoring API
interfaces.description: The system will use this API to receive real-time transaction data and monitor for suspicious patterns.

interfaces.name: Fraud Analyst Dashboard
interfaces.description: This interface will be used by fraud analysts to review flagged transactions and update the system status.

test_cases.description: Multiple rapid withdrawals within a predefined time frame are detected.
test_cases.expected_result: The system flags all transactions in the sequence for review.

test_cases.description: Normal transaction volume is monitored with no suspicious activity.
test_cases.expected_result: The system detects no flagged transactions.
The system logs the outage and resumes operation when network is restored, without missing any transactions.
